mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 0.3% MoM are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor market slack and underemployment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to generate a negative predictive IC over 60-day horizons in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >60% of triggered months.
